hotspots.title: Meloni’s labor decree and Minetti pardon scandal
hotspots.blurb: Italy’s government faces intense scrutiny over a controversial labor decree and the pardon of Nicole Minetti, a former showgirl linked to Silvio Berlusconi. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s defense of Justice Minister Carlo Nordio amid allegations of procedural irregularities has sparked outrage and calls for resignations.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the moral and political stakes of judicial integrity, leadership accountability, and the government’s handling of high-profile clemency cases.

hotspots.title: Trump’s WHCD evacuation after shooting
hotspots.blurb: The White House Correspondents’ Dinner was disrupted by an armed individual attempting to breach security, leading to an evacuation. The incident has reignited debates over White House security protocols and sparked political fallout, including calls for enhanced protections and criticism of media coverage.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the high-stakes security implications, the symbolic vulnerability of the presidency, and the polarized reactions to media and political responses.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s conservative People’s Party (PP) and far-right Vox have formed regional alliances under the banner of ‘national priority,’ sparking debates over immigration, healthcare access, and social welfare policies. The pacts have led to clashes in Congress and protests, with critics accusing the coalition of undermining constitutional principles.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the charged rhetoric around national identity, resource allocation, and the constitutional implications of prioritizing citizens over immigrants in public services.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) has surged in polls, overtaking the ruling CDU/CSU coalition. The party’s growing influence has sparked debates over democratic resilience, constitutional loyalty, and the response of mainstream parties to its extremist rhetoric.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the existential threat AfD’s rise poses to Germany’s democratic institutions, the moral panic over extremism, and the polarized reactions from political and civil society.

hotspots.title: Tisza’s victory and Fidesz’s post-Orbán reshuffle
hotspots.blurb: Hungary’s opposition Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, won a landslide victory in the April 2026 elections, ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. The defeat has triggered internal turmoil within Fidesz, including Orbán’s offer to resign as party leader and debates over the party’s future direction.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the historic shift in Hungarian politics, the symbolic fall of Orbán’s authoritarian regime, and the intense intra-party conflicts over leadership and policy direction.
